name: Tabucom
description: Publishes HTML, Markdown, or prebuilt static-site ZIPs at immutable URLs with optional single-password protection and expiry.
instructions: Publish only the final static artifact that a browser can serve directly. Do not upload project source files. Use the origin serving this document as the publish origin; operators should set PUBLIC_API_URL so generated docs and API responses use the externally reachable URL. Publish built static output from directories such as dist/, build/, out/, or public/. If no build output exists, only run an obvious build command such as npm run build, pnpm build, or yarn build. For ZIP uploads, zip the build directory contents so index.html is at the ZIP root, use spa=1 for client-side routers, optionally use prefix=<slug> for a readable deployment ID, and on success return only url, expiresAt, protected, and password if returned.{{AGENT_API_KEY_INSTRUCTIONS}}{{AGENT_TOKEN_INSTRUCTIONS}} On failure, read the HTTP status and response body, explain the cause briefly, and suggest the smallest correction.

raw_source.description: Append raw=1 to a published deployment URL to retrieve the resolved stored file without Tabucom's browser copy control. HTML responses are byte-exact source for agent handoff and obey the same expiry and password gates as normal viewing.

constraints: Static output only; uploaded code is not executed.
ZIP archives must contain index.html at the root.
Never upload source directories, node_modules, .git, .env, secrets, or backend/server code.
Always use the URL returned by the publish response.
